page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates one physical core with two threads per core. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It features dedicated CPU allocation and lacks local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is a notable constraint, with Passmark memory scores and cached read-only small block memory bandwidth testing in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance, while complex workloads like software compilation and ray tracing register in the bottom 10%.

The c2d-standard-2 offers an entry-level compute profile with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. While single-core execution is relatively strong, the limited dual-thread capacity on a single physical core restricts its utility for highly parallelized tasks. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform poorly, reflecting the memory bandwidth limitations. This instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and low-intensity background tasks that do not require high memory throughput or local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is an entry-level compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it provides a single physical core with two threads and dedicated CPU allocation. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng tests. However, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower percentiles, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10%. Given these performance characteristics, the c2d-standard-2 is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and low-concurrency application hosting rather than memory-intensive databases or parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is a 2-vCPU, 8 GB RAM virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 features 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong single-core compute performance.
tagline: An entry-level x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC resources, strong single-core execution, and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM
Dedicated CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 2 threads
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic web serving
